uncoveredSurfaceSemantics.partial: A partial surface must cite existing deterministic evidence, retain one or more coverage gaps, and still be missing at least one required planned-proof lane or planned deterministic fixture.
uncoveredSurfaceSemantics.uncovered: An uncovered surface has scoped contract metadata but no currently implemented required proof lane and must enumerate the missing deterministic fixture/report path needed for downstream work.
uncoveredSurfaceSemantics.covered: A covered surface is only truthful when every required lane is already implemented and no coverage gaps or planned deterministic fixtures remain.

proofClasses.name: report-contract
proofClasses.description: Machine-readable release/report surfaces that downstream gates can consume without rediscovering the underlying tests.

proofClasses.name: integration-contract
proofClasses.description: Deterministic integration tests that lock scoped behavior for a secondary surface.

proofClasses.name: unit-contract
proofClasses.description: Deterministic unit/schema contracts for lower-level secondary surfaces such as MCP.

proofClasses.name: repo-recording
proofClasses.description: Representative recorded repo/dev proof intended to show a realistic secondary-surface parity path without live reruns.

proofClasses.name: installed-recording
proofClasses.description: Representative recorded packaged/install proof intended to show the same parity surface under installed mode where required.

lanes.name: secondary-parity-report
lanes.title: Release-readable secondary parity report
lanes.description: Publishes the machine-readable report row that downstream release gates consume for web-mode parity.

lanes.name: integration:web-mode
lanes.title: Web-mode integration contract lane
lanes.description: Uses the tracked web-mode integration tests as the current deterministic proof lane for launch, onboarding, and session/recovery command-surface behavior.

lanes.name: repo-recording:web-mode
lanes.title: Representative repo-mode web recording
lanes.description: Defines a future deterministic repo/dev recording for browser-only web mode beyond the current integration contracts.

lanes.name: installed-recording:web-mode
lanes.title: Representative installed-mode web recording
lanes.description: Defines a future packaged web-mode parity recording if the matrix needs repo/install dual proof instead of a documented exception.

lanes.name: secondary-parity-report:mcp
lanes.title: Release-readable MCP parity report
lanes.description: Publishes the machine-readable report row that downstream release gates consume for MCP parity.

lanes.name: unit:mcp
lanes.title: MCP contract/unit lane
lanes.description: Uses the existing deterministic MCP server and client schema tests as the current scoped proof lane.

lanes.name: integration:mcp-session
lanes.title: Representative MCP interaction lane
lanes.description: Defines a future representative MCP client/server interaction recording for parity closure.

lanes.name: secondary-parity-report:workflow-bmad
lanes.title: Release-readable workflow parity report
lanes.description: Publishes the machine-readable report row that downstream release gates consume for representative workflow/BMAD parity.

lanes.name: integration:workflow-contracts
lanes.title: Workflow action contract lane
lanes.description: Uses tracked web workflow control/action contracts and command-routing coverage as the current deterministic workflow proof lane.

lanes.name: repo-recording:workflow-bmad
lanes.title: Representative workflow fixture lane
lanes.description: Defines a future deterministic workflow/BMAD fixture proving one representative end-to-end guided flow.

lanes.name: secondary-parity-report:worktree-session-recovery
lanes.title: Release-readable worktree/session parity report
lanes.description: Publishes the machine-readable report row that downstream release gates consume for worktree/session/recovery parity.

lanes.name: integration:session-recovery
lanes.title: Session and recovery integration lane
lanes.description: Uses tracked session, resume, headless, and web recovery diagnostics contracts as the current deterministic parity lane.

lanes.name: installed-recording:worktree-session-recovery
lanes.title: Representative installed worktree/session recording
lanes.description: Defines a future packaged representative worktree/session recovery recording if parity closure requires installed-mode proof beyond contract coverage.

surfaces.title: Browser-only web mode parity surface
surfaces.scopeBoundary: Covers the shipped `umb --web` / `umb web` launch path, host/network flags, onboarding lock state, command-surface parity, and recovery/session diagnostics exposed in the web UI. It does not yet prove a dedicated parity fixture through the release-gate artifact.

deterministicFixtures.title: Secondary surface inventory artifact
deterministicFixtures.purpose: Provides the truthful current-state audit row that anchors web-mode parity scope and known gaps.

deterministicFixtures.title: Web-mode integration contract
deterministicFixtures.purpose: Locks the current deterministic launch/path/behavior contract for web mode.

deterministicFixtures.title: Future release-readable web-mode parity row
deterministicFixtures.purpose: Reserved output path for the downstream release-readable web-mode report row.

coverageGaps.summary: No dedicated secondary-surface parity artifact reports web-mode coverage in the parity release workflow.
coverageGaps.downstreamNeed: Publish a machine-readable lane/report entry so release-gate consumers can read web-mode parity without re-inferring it from individual tests.

coverageGaps.summary: Current web-mode evidence is integration-heavy but not framed as installed-mode parity proof alongside repo-mode parity.
coverageGaps.downstreamNeed: Define whether web mode needs repo/install dual proof or an explicit scoped exception in the parity matrix.

surfaces.title: MCP server and client parity surface
surfaces.scopeBoundary: Covers `--mode mcp` startup, packaged MCP module importability, bundled MCP extension conflicts, and MCP-related package server contracts. It does not yet prove an end-to-end secondary parity lane that a downstream release gate can read as MCP parity.

deterministicFixtures.title: Secondary surface inventory artifact
deterministicFixtures.purpose: Provides the truthful current-state audit row that anchors MCP parity scope and known gaps.

deterministicFixtures.title: MCP schema contract
deterministicFixtures.purpose: Locks the deterministic MCP tool-schema contract already shipping in-repo.

deterministicFixtures.title: MCP parity artifact
deterministicFixtures.purpose: Tracked release-readable MCP parity artifact proving discovery, schema, success, and failure diagnostics.

coverageGaps.summary: No secondary parity lane currently emits MCP-specific covered/uncovered status into a tracked parity artifact.
coverageGaps.downstreamNeed: Add a deterministic MCP parity contract/report lane so downstream slices stop treating MCP support as an implicit side effect of unit tests.

coverageGaps.summary: Existing MCP tests do not yet demonstrate the installed packaged CLI serving a representative MCP parity interaction in the parity report surface.
coverageGaps.downstreamNeed: Define a representative MCP interaction and attach it to the parity matrix.

surfaces.title: Workflow / BMAD representative parity surface
surfaces.scopeBoundary: Covers representative workflow command routing, workflow CTA execution, and the shipped skill/workflow assets that shape guided flows. It does not yet include a deterministic parity fixture that proves a representative workflow/BMAD loop end to end.

deterministicFixtures.title: Secondary surface inventory artifact
deterministicFixtures.purpose: Provides the truthful current-state audit row that anchors workflow/BMAD parity scope and known gaps.

deterministicFixtures.title: Workflow action execution contract
deterministicFixtures.purpose: Locks the deterministic workflow action plumbing already present in the repo.

deterministicFixtures.title: Representative workflow parity fixture manifest
deterministicFixtures.purpose: Tracked representative workflow/BMAD manifest path for the deterministic planning-to-execution parity loop.

coverageGaps.summary: No tracked parity fixture yet exercises one representative workflow/BMAD path as a release-readable contract.
coverageGaps.downstreamNeed: Define a deterministic workflow fixture or manifest entry so the parity report can state exactly what workflow parity means.

coverageGaps.summary: BMAD-relevant workflow coverage exists only indirectly through command/action contracts and shipped workflow assets.
coverageGaps.downstreamNeed: Publish explicit scope semantics describing which workflow/BMAD experiences are in or out of parity scope.

surfaces.title: Worktree, session, and recovery parity surface
surfaces.scopeBoundary: Covers CLI session listing/resume, headless resume semantics, worktree subcommands/help, and web command-surface recovery/session diagnostics. It does not yet publish a dedicated parity artifact that unifies those proofs and the remaining drift points.

deterministicFixtures.title: Secondary surface inventory artifact
deterministicFixtures.purpose: Provides the truthful current-state audit row that anchors worktree/session/recovery parity scope and known gaps.

deterministicFixtures.title: Session and recovery integration contract
deterministicFixtures.purpose: Locks deterministic session-browser, resume, rename, and settings contracts already tracked in-repo.

deterministicFixtures.title: Future installed worktree/session recovery recording
deterministicFixtures.purpose: Reserved tracked recording path for a representative installed worktree/session recovery proof.

coverageGaps.summary: Worktree/session/recovery evidence is scattered across CLI and web tests but not summarized in a parity artifact.
coverageGaps.downstreamNeed: Publish one machine-readable matrix row that downstream slices and release gates can consume directly.

coverageGaps.summary: There is no deterministic installed-mode parity fixture demonstrating representative worktree/session recovery behavior beyond individual contract tests.
coverageGaps.downstreamNeed: Decide whether a worktree/session fixture is required or whether scoped contract coverage is sufficient for parity closure.
